metadata.description:
Batch execution of 8 literature research reports using the create-literature-report skill v2.1.0.
PRIMARY (R1-R4): User's 4 themes from research-reports-general-plan-for-manuscript-writing.md SUPPLEMENTARY (R5-R8): Additional themes identified during session analysis
Each report targets a distinct theme with NO overlap with the existing 9th report (biomedical-agentic-ai-research-report.md).
DUAL MANDATE: 1. Inform CODE development (KG updates, OpenSpec specs, GitHub issues) 2. Inform MANUSCRIPT writing (neurovex-discussion.tex future work sections)
The knowledge graph is the GROUND TRUTH. Code and .tex manuscripts are downstream projections from the KG.

theme.name: computational-reproducibility
theme.title: Computational Reproducibility in Science and Biomedical ML
theme.unique-angle: Reproducibility crisis, Jupyter failures, Docker vulnerabilities, pinned deps, DVC, MLflow, code smells. Position NEUROVEX as a solution.

theme.name: medical-mlops
theme.title: Medical MLOps: From Preclinical Research to Clinical Regulated Deployment
theme.unique-angle: Full MLOps maturity spectrum, QMSR, PCCP, SaMD, IEC 62304, OpenLineage audit trails, SBOM, post-market surveillance, MedMLOps 4 pillars.

theme.name: biomedical-segmentation
theme.title: Biomedical Segmentation: Tubular Structures, Foundation Models, and Statistical Evaluation
theme.unique-angle: Tubular segmentation + MONAI, SAM3/VesselFM foundation models, calibration/UQ, TRIPOD+AI/PROBAST+AI, factorial design in imaging, sample size.

theme.name: multiphoton-neuroscience
theme.title: Multiphoton Microscopy for Neuroscience: Smart Acquisition and Closed-Loop Feedback
theme.unique-angle: 2-photon smart acquisition, closed-loop neural feedback in vivo, OME-Zarr data infrastructure, real-time decoding, dose reduction.

theme.name: post-training-methods
theme.title: Post-Training Methodologies for Medical Segmentation
theme.unique-angle: SWA variants, model soup theory, temperature scaling, Platt scaling, isotonic regression, conformal calibration — specifically on SMALL datasets (70 volumes) with 3D medical segmentation. How post-training transforms interact with loss function choice (cbdice_cldice vs dice_ce). What the factorial design should measure for post-training factors.
theme.excluded-from: Agentic AI (covered in report 5). Ensemble methods (covered in R2). Federated learning (covered in R3). Regulatory (covered in R4).

theme.name: ensemble-uncertainty
theme.title: Ensemble Strategies and Uncertainty Quantification for Biomedical Segmentation
theme.unique-angle: Deep ensembles vs model merging vs model soup. MC Dropout vs conformal prediction vs evidential deep learning. Calibration metrics (Brier, ECE, reliability diagrams). How to select the champion model from a factorial design. Ensemble diversity vs accuracy tradeoff on small medical datasets.
theme.excluded-from: Post-training methods like SWA (covered in R1). Agentic AI (report 5). Federated learning (R3). Regulatory compliance (R4).
theme.manuscript-section: Methods: Evaluation Pipeline (R2c), Results: Multi-Model Comparison (R3b)

theme.name: federated-multisite
theme.title: Federated Learning and Multi-Site Data Architecture for Preclinical Neurovascular Imaging
theme.unique-angle: MONAI FL vs NVIDIA FLARE. Privacy-preserving multi-institutional training for 2-photon microscopy data. Domain shift between imaging sites (different microscopes, staining, species). Opt-in data pooling architecture. How federated learning changes the factorial design (site as an additional factor). Preclinical vs clinical FL differences.
theme.excluded-from: Single-site training methods (R1, R2). Agentic AI (report 5). Regulatory compliance details (R4). FDA post-market (R4).

theme.name: regulatory-postmarket
theme.title: Regulatory Compliance and Post-Market Surveillance for MLOps-Driven Medical AI
theme.unique-angle: QMSR (Feb 2026) + PCCP + IEC 62304 + TRIPOD+AI mapped to MLOps pipelines. OpenLineage as IEC 62304 Clause 8 audit trail. SBOM (CycloneDX) as FDA requirement. Post-market surveillance via drift detection + retraining triggers. How our factorial design IS a PCCP template. The locked→adaptive model lifecycle.
theme.excluded-from: Agentic AI governance (report 5 §5). Post-training methods (R1). Ensemble selection (R2). Multi-site data (R3).

coordination.dedup-rule: Before writing each report, read ALL previously completed reports (including biomedical-agentic-ai-research-report.md). If a paper appears in a previous report, do NOT include it again — reference it as "see Report N" instead. Each report must contribute UNIQUE papers not cited elsewhere.
coordination.kg-update-protocol:
After each report, identify: 1. NEW decision nodes needed (create YAML files) 2. EXISTING nodes that need posterior updates (cite new evidence) 3. NEW edges between nodes revealed by the research 4. STALE nodes that the research contradicts
Write updates to workspace/kg-updates-{theme_id}.yaml for review.
coordination.manuscript-integration: Each report's §6 (Discussion/Future Work) feeds directly into neurovex-discussion.tex. The discussion section should reference specific KG decision nodes and their posteriors as evidence.
coordination.openspec-integration: Each report's §7 (Recommended Issues) should map to OpenSpec change proposals where appropriate. P0/P1 issues that affect the factorial experiment should be flagged as BLOCKING.

execution.order: R1 → R2 → R3 → R4 (primary) then optionally R5 → R6 → R7 → R8 (supplementary)
execution.rationale:
PRIMARY (user's themes): R1 (reproducibility) provides the "why" for the entire platform. R2 (medical MLOps) builds on R1 with regulatory specifics. R3 (biomedical segmentation) covers the core science and methods. R4 (multiphoton neuroscience) provides domain-specific context.
SUPPLEMENTARY (session-identified gaps): R5 (post-training) deepens R3's post-training section. R6 (ensemble) deepens R3's evaluation section. R7 (federated) deepens R2's multi-site aspects. R8 (regulatory) deepens R2's FDA compliance specifics.
execution.per-report-steps: For each report Rn: 1. Open new Claude Code session (fresh context) 2. Paste: "create literature report on {theme.title}" 3. Provide seed papers from {child-plan} 4. Skill executes: CAPTURE → GATHER → SYNTHESIZE → VERIFY → DELIVER 5. Review output, commit, push 6. Update KG with findings
execution.time-budget: Per report: ~60 minutes (skill pipeline) Total: ~4 hours for all 4 reports KG updates: ~30 minutes after all reports

runner-script.description:
Simple sequential .sh script. NO screen, NO tmux, NO background processes. Each report runs as a separate `claude -p` invocation that blocks until complete. Output logged to individual .log files.
WARNING: These scripts have historically frozen (metalearning 2026-03-09, 2026-03-16). The RECOMMENDED approach is manual execution of each report in a fresh Claude Code session.
